county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Chillicothe. One of Ohio's earliest counties (1798). Chillicothe was Ohio's first state capital (1803-1810, 1812-1816). Located in the Virginia Military District — VA bounty land warrants essential. Ross County Historical Society. Hopewell Culture National Historical Park. Adena Mansion and Gardens. First Ohio constitutional convention records. Paint Creek valley. Chillicothe Gazette (1800+) is one of Ohio's oldest newspapers. Camp Sherman (WWI) records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1798. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Ross County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1798. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Ross County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Ross County Clerk of Courts, Chillicothe
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1798. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Ross County Auditor, Chillicothe / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1798. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Ross County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

source_profiles.repository: Ross County Clerk of Courts, Chillicothe / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Ross County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Fremont. Created 1820 from Huron. Located in the Firelands (Connecticut Western Reserve fire sufferers grants). Rutherford B. Hayes Presidential Library and Museums in Fremont — major research center with local family papers. Sandusky County Historical Society. Birchard Public Library local history. Fort Stephenson (War of 1812) battle records. Lower Sandusky was an important fur trading post. Strong German immigrant community.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Portsmouth. Created 1803 from Adams County. Located on the Ohio River at the mouth of the Scioto River. Ohio & Erie Canal terminus. Portsmouth was a major river port — steamboat records. Scioto County Historical Society. Boneyfiddle historic district. Scioto Trail heritage. Floodwall murals document local history. Southern Ohio iron furnace records. Shawnee State Forest area. Strong African American community along the river.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1803. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.

source_profiles.source_name: Hanging Rock Iron Region Records — Scioto County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Charcoal iron furnace records including worker rolls, company store ledgers, and furnace operation logs. The Hanging Rock Iron Region in southern Ohio employed thousands of workers including Welsh, English, and African American laborers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered — some at Ohio History Connection, some at local museums (e.g., Buckeye Furnace State Memorial)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Tiffin. Created 1820 from Huron County. Named for the Seneca people (Mingo) — check NARA for removal-era records (Seneca removal 1831). Heidelberg University (1850) archives in Tiffin. Seneca County Historical Society. Located in the Firelands area. Tiffin University archives. Fort Ball and Fort Seneca (War of 1812) records. Strong German immigrant community. Green Springs health resort records (1830s-1860s). Tiffin Glass factory records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Sidney. Created 1819 from Miami County. Located in Congress Lands. Shelby County Historical Society in Sidney. Monarch Machine Tool Company and other factory employment records. Ross County was an early administrative center for this area. Strong German Catholic heritage in Anna, Botkins, and Jackson Center areas. Great Miami River valley. Check Miami County for pre-1819 records. Shelby County airport area.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

source_profiles.source_name: German Catholic Parish Records — Shelby County
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archdiocese of Cincinnati / Parish offices
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptism, marriage, and burial records from German Catholic parishes. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. Archdiocese of Cincinnati central archives hold some consolidated records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Canton. Created 1808 from Columbiana. Canton is a major city — Pro Football Hall of Fame city. Stark County Historical Society. McKinley Presidential Library and Museum. Hoover vacuum company records in North Canton. Timken Company records in Canton. Strong German, Irish, and Italian immigrant communities. Massillon is a significant industrial city — steel industry records. Tuscarawas River area. Well-preserved early county records from 1809.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Akron. Created 1840 from Medina, Portage, and Stark. Western Reserve county (partially). Akron was the Rubber Capital of the World — Goodyear, Firestone, B.F. Goodrich, and General Tire employee records document thousands of families. Massive African American migration from the South (1910s-1950s). Summit County Historical Society (Perkins Stone Mansion). University of Akron archives. Ohio & Erie Canal records. John Brown house (abolitionist). Check parent counties for pre-1840 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1840. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Western Reserve Historical Society, Cleveland / CT State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Connecticut ceded its Western Reserve to the federal government in 1800, but settlers arrived from 1796. Connecticut Land Company records document original purchasers and settlers. Check Connecticut state records for settler origins.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at WRHS in Cleveland; Connecticut State Library also has copies

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Warren. One of the earliest Western Reserve counties (1800). Named for Jonathan Trumbull, CT governor. Check Connecticut records for early settlers. Western Reserve Heritage Foundation. Packard Electric (General Motors) records in Warren. Trumbull County Historical Society. Steel industry in Warren and Niles area. William McKinley birthplace in Niles. Mahoning River valley. Strong early Congregational and Presbyterian church records reflecting CT heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1800. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in New Philadelphia. Created 1808 from Muskingum. Named for the Tuscarawas River. Important Moravian missionary history — Schoenbrunn Village (1772) was Ohio's first settlement; Gnadenhutten massacre (1782) records. Tuscarawas County Historical Society. Zoar Village (German Separatist community, 1817-1898) records at Ohio History Connection. Dover/New Philadelphia twin cities. Congress Lands (U.S. Military District). Strong German heritage. Canal Dover records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1808. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

source_profiles.source_name: Amish and Mennonite Church Records — Tuscarawas County
source_profiles.repository: Heritage Historical Library, Aylmer ON / Mennonite Historical Library, Goshen IN / Local church records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Amish church district records, family group sheets, Budget newspaper correspondence, and Mennonite church records. Ohio has the largest Amish population in the world. Records may be in German/Pennsylvanian Dutch. Die Botschaft and The Budget newspapers document Amish community events.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Amish records remain in private family collections; Heritage Historical Library has compiled indexes

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1820 from Delaware, Franklin, Logan, Madison.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Marysville. Created 1820 from Delaware, Franklin, Logan, and Madison. Located partly in Virginia Military District and Congress Lands. Union County Historical Society. Marysville named for the wife of Samuel W. Cuthbertson, town founder. Honda of America (1979) brought later growth. Jerome Township and Milford Center area early records. Agricultural county. Check all four parent counties for pre-1820 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Union County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Union County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Union County Clerk of Courts, Marysville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1820. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Union County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA
